summary: Drew DeVault announces the sway 1.0-beta.1 release, marking a major milestone after 1,173 days and over 8,000 commits. The release achieves 100% compatibility with the i3 window manager while running on Wayland, and represents a complete rewrite of sway's internals built on the wlroots library. The post highlights new features including improved output handling, HiDPI support, multi-GPU support, advanced input configuration, and a growing ecosystem of third-party tools built on standardized protocols. DeVault credits the community of contributors and positions sway as the most fully featured and stable Wayland desktop available.
key_points: Sway 1.0-beta.1 achieves 100% i3 compatibility on Wayland, with a complete internal rewrite powered by wlroots, now the basis of 10 Wayland compositors
New output features include display rotation, improved HiDPI and fractional scaling, multi-GPU support, and daisy-chained DisplayPort configurations
The layer-shell protocol enables a growing ecosystem of interoperable third-party tools like panels, notification daemons, screen capture utilities, and even a phone shell
Advanced input features now support drawing tablets, multiple independent cursors and keyboards, and flexible keyboard layout configuration
The release includes idle management, DPMS support, drag-and-drop, atomic layout updates, and clipboard syncing between Wayland and X11
money_quotes: What started as a side project became the most fully featured and stable Wayland desktop available, and drove the development of what has become the dominant solution for building Wayland compositors.
The difference between sway 0.15 and sway 1.0 is like night and day.
One of our main commitments to you for sway 1.0 and wlroots is to break the boundaries between Wayland compositors and encourage standard interoperable protocols - and we've done so.
Sway now has the best HiDPI support on Linux, period.
When we started, I honestly didn't think we'd get here.
It's only thanks to an amazing team of talented contributors that we did.
You can even give your coworker the ability to focus and type into separate windows from what you're working on.
key_insight: Sway's 1.0 beta demonstrates that a community-driven open source project can deliver a polished, feature-complete Wayland desktop while simultaneously creating reusable infrastructure (wlroots) that benefits the entire ecosystem.
